name: Vehicle Acceleration
description: Vehicle Acceleration is a controllable transport mechanic with dedicated movement, physics, resources, or damage behavior.
design_notes: Vehicle Acceleration should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For vehicles mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use vehicle acceleration as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect vehicle acceleration to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: The player feels mastery over a machine with distinct weight, speed, and handling.

parameters.name: max_speed
parameters.description: Maximum velocity the vehicle can reach.

parameters.name: handling_response
parameters.description: How sharply steering or control input affects the vehicle.

parameters.name: traction
parameters.description: Grip coefficient used for braking, turning, or drifting.

edge_cases: Vehicle Acceleration is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Vehicle Acceleration overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Vehicle Acceleration runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Vehicle Acceleration state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Vehicle Acceleration plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Vehicle Acceleration produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Vehicle Acceleration strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Vehicle Acceleration becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Vehicle Acceleration with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Vehicle Acceleration as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Vehicle Acceleration with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Vehicle Acceleration as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.
